platformMix.note: Viewer share uses GA4 active users grouped by stream and device data where available. People who use more than one platform may appear in more than one bucket. Apple TV is not separately broken out yet; Amazon/Fire TV is inferred from Amazon AFT smart-TV device models until the developer confirms a cleaner Fire TV label.

groups.detail: Browser and web playback from the electricnow-web GA4 stream.
iPhone and iPad app activity; Apple TV may also be included here until it is tagged separately.
Android phone/tablet activity from the ElectricNOW Android GA4 stream.
Roku activity from the electricnow-roku GA4 stream.
Likely Fire TV activity inferred from Amazon AFT smart-TV model codes.
Android TV / Google TV stream activity, excluding likely Fire TV devices where GA4 exposes Amazon AFT models.
Not separately broken out yet. It is likely inside the Apple app stream until the developer sends a distinct Apple TV platform or stream.

plainEnglishInsights: Measured ElectricNOW platform audience was 4.7K GA4 active users excluding landing-page acquisition paths, down 35.0% from the previous week.
Acquisition traffic is tracked separately: property-wide GA4 active users including landing-page traffic were 4.8K, down 41.1% from the previous week; landing-page users were down 77.7%.

Measured Apple/Android app-stream usage improved: 2.6K active users this week, up 67.4% from launch baseline and down 18.1% from the previous week; Apple TV is not yet split out from the Apple/iOS stream.
Viewing also improved: 1.1K app users watched on-demand or Live content, up 56.6% from launch baseline and down 25.4% from the previous week.
Roughly 44.1% of app users watched something, compared with 47.2% during launch week and 48.5% the prior week, so the viewing conversion rate is steady while the audience is growing.
On-demand is carrying most of the viewing growth with 1.1K starter users; Live remains meaningful at 34 viewers, down 26.1% from launch baseline and down 42.4% from the previous week.
Actual viewing behavior is measured separately: 1.5K users triggered a viewing event, down 27.1% from the previous week; video-complete users were down 16.0%.
Average time on platform is 62.4 engaged minutes per active user across all GA4-tracked platforms, up 37.3 minutes from launch week and up 22.8 minutes from the previous week.
Read this as two different measures: average time per user shows total weekly attention per person, while average time per session shows how long each individual visit or viewing session lasted.
Stripe-visible sales show 1 payments and $14.99 last week, plus 0 payments and $0.00 so far this week; this excludes Apple App Store and Google Play purchases.
Top watched programs in GA4 this week include ElectricNOW, Leverage, The Ark, Almost Paradise, and Leverage: Redemption.
The most watched Live channels generated 3.9K Live play events, led by ElectricNOW, ElectricNOW en Español, Daily Flash, Kung Fu Movies, and AMPD TV.
GA4 and Stripe should still be reconciled with Apple App Store, Google Play, Amagi, ad-server, YouTube, and Meta reporting before making final business decisions.
instrumentationGaps: No separate GA4 event found for go_ad_free_click yet.
No separate GA4 event found for video_purchase yet.
No separate GA4 event found for collection_purchase yet.

Stripe is now one visible sales source in the dashboard; total sales still require Apple App Store and Google Play reporting, and GA4 purchase events still need to be split into go-ad-free, individual video, and collection purchase actions.
Live TV should be reconciled with Amagi and ad-server reporting.

salesSummary.sourceDetail: This shows Stripe-visible sales from the connected electricnow.tv account only. It is not total ElectricNOW sales because Apple App Store and Google Play purchases are handled separately.

salesSummary.note: Stripe-visible sales show 11 payments and $159.89 year to date, 1 payment and $14.99 this month, 1 payment and $14.99 last week, and 0 payments and $0.00 this week. Add Apple App Store and Google Play reports before treating this as total sales.

live.note: Live usage is based on GA4 screen name 'Live' plus playback events that also carried the Live screen name.

onDemand.note: On Demand usage is based on GA4 screen name 'On Demand'. Title-level viewing is shown below from GA4 video_title play events.

titleViewership.note: Top programs roll up fragmented GA4 play-event labels into parent shows/collections where possible, then use a GA4 filter to estimate deduped active users for each program. Play events remain the ranking metric because ads monetize playback; active users are total GA4 users who triggered matching play events for that program during the period. Treat this as GA-tracked viewership, not a fully cleaned catalog report yet, until the app sends content_type, series_title, season, episode, and video_id consistently.

metaCampaignContext.note: Meta campaign objective shifted from landing-page traffic, to app-store button-click leads, to direct app-install campaigns starting Apr 28. The app-install phase bypasses the website landing page, so lower landing-page traffic is expected and should not be judged as a standalone negative.
metaCampaignContext.appInstallBreakout: Through May 3, Meta reported 516 iOS installs at about $0.65 each and 949 Google Play installs at about $0.41 each.
metaCampaignContext.interpretation: Judge the newest phase by installs, app opens, viewing, Live usage, purchases, and ad value rather than by landing-page traffic.

launchBaseline.note: Platform time is based on GA4 userEngagementDuration across tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths. Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

previousWeek.note: Platform time is based on GA4 userEngagementDuration across tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths. Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

Use this as the cleaner attention metric; GA4 average session duration is retained for context but may overstate streaming attention when sessions remain open.

scorecardGroups.title: Measured ElectricNOW Platform Audience
scorecardGroups.eyebrow: Is the measured platform growing or shrinking?
scorecardGroups.definition: GA4 active users across tracked ElectricNOW platform surfaces, excluding landing-page acquisition paths. Landing-page traffic is reported only in Web Acquisition. Reconcile with Amagi, app stores, ad servers, and platform dashboards before treating this as total company-wide audience.
